title: Perityle gracilis
intro: Perityle gracilis is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae), commonly known as a type of cactus yucca or a daisy bush. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Perityle gracilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Perityle gracilis is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine in Mexico and other parts of the Americas.
taxonomy: Perityle gracilis is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it within the tribe Heliantheae and the subtribe Peritylinae. At the genus level, Perityle gracilis belongs to the genus Perityle, which consists of various species of flowering plants. The species Perityle gracilis is specifically found in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Further classification within the species level is not yet well established.
